identity.name: Linux Persistence — Cron, Systemd, Backdoor User and SSH Keys (T1053.003)
identity.summary: An operator establishes multiple persistence footholds on a Linux host: a cron entry pointing at a hidden beacon, a systemd unit with Restart=always, a backdoor account added to the sudo group, an SSH authorized_keys plant, and a shell-profile login trigger. Cortex XDR for Linux detects each mechanism via file-write and process BIOCs and stitches them into one persistence incident.
identity.description: After gaining root on a Linux host, threat actors such as TeamTNT layer several persistence mechanisms so a single cleanup does not evict them. This scenario reproduces five Linux persistence techniques: a malicious cron job referencing a hidden binary, a systemd service unit with Restart=always and a /tmp ExecStart, a backdoor local account added to the sudo group, an SSH key plant into authorized_keys, and a shell-profile (.bashrc/.profile) login trigger with nohup. The detection approach combines process telemetry (crontab, useradd, ssh-keygen, systemctl) with file-write telemetry into the persistence-relevant paths (/etc/cron*, /etc/systemd/system, ~/.ssh/authorized_keys, shell init files). Each mechanism maps to a dedicated Cortex XDR BIOC; an analytics layer recognizes automated, non-interactive provisioning; and a correlation rule fuses any combination of these footholds into one persistence incident so the SOC sees the full picture rather than five disconnected alerts.

techniques.name: Create or Modify System Process: Systemd Service
Event Triggered Execution: Unix Shell Configuration Modification

execution.framework_reference_id: T1053.003 / T1543.002 / T1136.001 / T1098.004 — Linux persistence atomic tests

prerequisites.description: Cortex XDR Linux agent installed and reporting healthy on the target.
prerequisites.check_command: systemctl is-active cortex-agent || ps aux | grep -i traps

prerequisites.description: Root privilege on the target (useradd/systemd persistence require it).

payload.code:
# step-1: cron persistence (staged)
echo '*/15 * * * * /tmp/.cortexsim_beacon 2>/dev/null' > /tmp/cortexsim_cron_test
# step-2: systemd unit (staged)
printf '[Service]\nExecStart=/tmp/.cortexsim_beacon\nRestart=always\n' > /tmp/cortexsim-backdoor.service
# step-3: backdoor user
useradd -M -s /bin/bash -G sudo cortexsim_testuser 2>/dev/null && echo 'cortexsim_testuser:TempP@ss123' | chpasswd
# step-4: ssh key plant (staged)
ssh-keygen -t ed25519 -f /tmp/.cortexsim_ssh_test/id_ed25519 -N '' -q
# step-5: shell profile login trigger (staged)
echo 'nohup /tmp/.cortexsim_beacon &>/dev/null &' > /tmp/cortexsim_profile_test

expected_artifacts.description: Cron entry referencing a hidden /tmp beacon path.
Systemd unit with Restart=always and a /tmp ExecStart.
useradd adding an account to the sudo group.
ssh-keygen generating a key followed by authorized_keys write.
Shell profile modification with nohup background execution.

cleanup.code:
userdel cortexsim_testuser 2>/dev/null
rm -f /tmp/cortexsim_cron_test /tmp/cortexsim_cron_backup /tmp/cortexsim-backdoor.service /tmp/cortexsim_profile_test
rm -rf /tmp/.cortexsim_ssh_test
echo '[CortexSim] SIM-EDR-003 cleanup complete'

biocs.name: EDR-003 Cron Job Creation With Suspicious Command Path
biocs.description: Cron job creation with suspicious command path — persistence mechanism via scheduled task. crontab installs an entry, or a cron file is written referencing a hidden/temp binary.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START and actor_process_image_name = "crontab") or (event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path contains_any ("/etc/cron", "/var/spool/cron"))
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("/tmp/", "/dev/shm/", "/.", "curl", "wget") or action_file_path contains "cron"

biocs.name: EDR-003 Systemd Unit With Suspicious ExecStart
biocs.description: Systemd service unit creation with suspicious ExecStart path — persistence via system service. A .service file is written with Restart=always and an ExecStart in /tmp or /dev/shm.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE, ENUM.FILE_WRITE)
| filter action_file_path contains ".service"
| filter action_file_path contains_any ("/etc/systemd/system", "/usr/lib/systemd/system", "/.config/systemd", "/tmp/")

biocs.name: EDR-003 Local Account Created With Sudo Group
biocs.description: Local account creation with sudo group membership — backdoor account for persistent access. useradd/usermod adds an account to sudo/wheel/admin.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS
| filter event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("useradd", "usermod", "adduser")
| filter action_process_image_command_line contains_any ("sudo", "wheel", "admin", "root")

biocs.name: EDR-003 SSH Key Generation Then Authorized Keys Write
biocs.description: SSH key generation followed by authorized_keys write — key-based persistence installation. ssh-keygen runs and an authorized_keys file is written.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter (event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START and actor_process_image_name = "ssh-keygen") or (event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE, ENUM.FILE_WRITE) and action_file_path contains "authorized_keys")

biocs.name: EDR-003 Shell Profile Modified With Background Execution
biocs.description: Shell profile modification with nohup background execution — login-triggered persistence. A shell init file is written containing nohup/disown background launch.
biocs.logic:
preset = xdr_data
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE
| filter event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE, ENUM.FILE_WRITE)
| filter action_file_path contains_any (".bashrc", ".bash_profile", ".profile", "/etc/profile", ".zshrc")
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("bash", "sh", "echo", "tee", "cat", "vi", "vim", "sed")

xql_queries.name: EDR-003 Analytics Crontab Modification Non-Interactive
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "crontab" or action_process_image_command_line contains "crontab"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line, causality_actor_process_image_name

xql_queries.name: EDR-003 Analytics Service Unit Restart Always Hidden Path
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and action_file_path contains ".service"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

xql_queries.name: EDR-003 Analytics User Account Creation Non-Login Session
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name in ("useradd", "adduser", "chpasswd")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-003 Analytics ssh-keygen Non-Interactive Persistence
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.PROCESS and event_sub_type = ENUM.PROCESS_START
| filter actor_process_image_name = "ssh-keygen"
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_effective_username, action_process_image_command_line

xql_queries.name: EDR-003 Analytics Shell Init File Modification
xql_queries.query:
preset = xdr_data
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter event_type = ENUM.FILE and event_sub_type in (ENUM.FILE_CREATE, ENUM.FILE_WRITE)
| filter action_file_path contains_any (".bashrc", ".profile", ".bash_profile", ".zshrc")
| fields _time, agent_hostname, actor_process_image_name, action_file_path

correlation_rules.name: Linux Multi-Mechanism Persistence Installation
correlation_rules.logic: BIOC(EDR-003 Cron Job Creation With Suspicious Command Path) OR BIOC(EDR-003 Systemd Unit With Suspicious ExecStart) OR BIOC(EDR-003 Local Account Created With Sudo Group) OR BIOC(EDR-003 SSH Key Generation Then Authorized Keys Write) OR BIOC(EDR-003 Shell Profile Modified With Background Execution)

analytics_modules: Cortex XDR Analytics — New persistence mechanism on host
Cortex XDR Analytics — Rare local account creation

products.evidence_query: preset = xdr_data | filter actor_process_image_name in ("crontab","useradd","ssh-keygen") | fields _time, agent_hostname, action_process_image_command_line
Correlation CR-EDR-0003 groups cron/systemd/user/ssh/profile persistence into one incident.
Playbook removes the cron entry, disables/deletes the unit, deletes the backdoor user, strips planted keys and reverts shell init files.

use_cases.name: Persistence Mechanism Detection — Linux
use_cases.description: Detect and evict layered Linux persistence (cron, systemd, backdoor user, SSH keys, shell profile) installed after compromise.

test_cases.objective: Each persistence mechanism fires its corresponding Cortex XDR BIOC.
preconditions: Cortex XDR Linux agent >= 8.4.0 installed and healthy.
BIOCs from this card imported and enabled.
steps: Observe per-mechanism BIOC alerts in the Cortex console.
success_criteria: BIOC `EDR-003 Local Account Created With Sudo Group` fires within 60s when useradd succeeds.
BIOC `EDR-003 SSH Key Generation Then Authorized Keys Write` fires within 60s.
All five XQL validation queries return >= 1 row.

test_cases.objective: Layered persistence stitches into one incident.
success_criteria: Correlation `CR-EDR-0003` groups the mechanisms on one host into a single persistence incident.
XSOAR eviction playbook is offered as a response action.

preventive_controls: Restrict cron and systemd unit directories to root and audit writes with auditd/fapolicyd.
Enforce centralized account management; alert on local account creation outside provisioning tooling.
Monitor ~/.ssh/authorized_keys integrity and disallow password-based SSH for service accounts.
Make shell init files immutable for service accounts where feasible.
detection_engineering: Alert on crontab/useradd/ssh-keygen run by non-interactive sessions or service accounts.
Watch .service file writes that include Restart=always with an ExecStart outside trusted paths.
Correlate multiple persistence writes on one host within a short window into a single incident.

references.title: Unit42 - TeamTNT Targets Exposed Docker APIs
